title: EV Charging Stations
description: Optional module to assess EV charging station physical security, safety interfaces, and operational integration.

module_questions.text: Is physical access to charging station equipment and control cabinets restricted to authorized personnel?
Are there mechanisms to detect and report tampering or unauthorized access at charging station equipment enclosures and control cabinets?
Is adequate lighting provided at charging station locations to support visibility and deter vandalism?
Are charging station cables and hardware secured against tampering and physical damage?
Are procedures in place to maintain controlled access at charging station access points during power outages or service disruptions?
Are roles and responsibilities defined for coordinating physical security response at charging stations when charging infrastructure systems are disrupted?

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Implement RFID access control at charging station equipment areas to restrict physical access to authorized personnel only.

sources.url: Cyber Threats to EV Charging Infrastructure - SEC Consult (January 15, 2025)
sources.label: Cyber Threats to EV Charging Infrastructure - SEC Consult (January 15, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Install surveillance cameras at charging station locations to monitor for unauthorized access and tampering.

sources.url: Electric Vehicle Charger Security Risks - VicOne (August 26, 2025)
sources.label: Electric Vehicle Charger Security Risks - VicOne (August 26, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Conduct regular security audits of charging station equipment and access controls to identify vulnerabilities.

sources.url: Sandia Studies Vulnerabilities of Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure (November 15, 2022)
sources.label: Sandia Studies Vulnerabilities of Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure (November 15, 2022)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Enhance lighting around charging stations to improve visibility and deter vandalism or theft.

sources.url: Improving Public EV Charger Locations - GreenTech (March 12, 2025)
sources.label: Improving Public EV Charger Locations - GreenTech (March 12, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Design charging station locations with visibility in mind, avoiding blind spots that could attract criminal activity.

sources.url: Public Space Design - Urban Planning Journal (July 18, 2023)
sources.label: Public Space Design - Urban Planning Journal (July 18, 2023)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Install panic buttons or alert systems at charging station locations to enable rapid response to security incidents.

sources.url: Modern Security Solutions for EV Chargers - TechSecure (June 21, 2024)
sources.label: Modern Security Solutions for EV Chargers - TechSecure (June 21, 2024)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Use tamper-proof hardware and secure enclosures for charging station equipment to prevent physical tampering.

sources.url: How to Address the Increasing Threat of Cyberattacks on EV Charging Stations - Virta (May 14, 2025)
sources.label: How to Address the Increasing Threat of Cyberattacks on EV Charging Stations - Virta (May 14, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Perform regular maintenance checks of charging station equipment to identify signs of tampering or damage.

sources.url: Charging Station Security Standards - Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Alliance (March 10, 2023)
sources.label: Charging Station Security Standards - Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Alliance (March 10, 2023)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Create awareness training for staff on identifying signs of tampering or unauthorized access at charging stations.

sources.url: The Importance of Staff Training in EV Charger Security - ChargeLab (October 02, 2024)
sources.label: The Importance of Staff Training in EV Charger Security - ChargeLab (October 02, 2024)

risk_drivers.driver_text: Data theft and skimming at charging stations may lead to identity theft and fraud, resulting in financial losses for users and damage to trust in the charging infrastructure.
